title: Invisible Injections: Unicode-based Adversarial Attacks on Tool-Integrated LLM Agents
contributions_summary: This paper introduces Unicode-based Stealthy Injection (USI), a novel adversarial attack paradigm that exploits the perception-semantic gap between human visual rendering and LLM tokenization to inject malicious payloads via invisible Unicode characters—such as zero-width characters, homoglyphs, tag characters, and bidirectional controls—while preserving the visual appearance of benign instructions. Through systematic evaluation across six major agent frameworks and state-of-the-art models, the authors demonstrate widespread vulnerability, achieving attack success rates exceeding 85% for semantic hijacking and inducing exponential latency spikes via context-window exhaustion. To counter this threat, they propose GPIGuard, a context-aware hybrid detection framework combining a rule-based Unicode codepoint scanner with a fine-tuned semantic verification model trained on a synthetic dataset of malicious and benign Unicode perturbations, which successfully identifies 74 harmful injections among 54,355 real-world prompts, validating both the stealth and prevalence of USI in practical deployments.
